sections.paragraph:
an unused root, i.q. Arab. زَرَّ to bind together, as with buckles, to buckle; a kindred root to זוּר No. I, also צָרַר, צוּר. Hence the nouns זֵר, זַרְזִיר. In Chaldee there occurs זְרַו to bind, originating in the quadril. זרזר.

II.

זרר

prop. to scatter; like the Arab. ذَرَّ: kindred roots זָרָה, זָרַע, זָרַק. Hence-

Poel זֹורֵר to sneeze, in doing which the particles of mucus are scattered from the nostrils, 2Ki 4:35. Comp. Ch. זְרֵיר sneezing; see Schult. ad Job 41:10.
